sprint: Companion Canon V2 -- Guardian & Creator Bond
task: A complete product-canon update, delivered as a frozen specification with the governing instruction: canon takes precedence over existing code, architecture stays generic. Corrects the earlier canon: Lumo is the Guardian of Story Companions, appearing only during Creator Ceremonies, never bonding with any Creator, never on a Magic Card. Every Creator receives exactly one randomly-bonded Story Companion (Nimbus the Dream Sprite, Quill the Ink Spirit -- two real registry entries seeded this sprint, art disclosed as pending) during a new, real, big centered Creator Ceremony stage (Story Egg Glow -> Cracks -> Lumo arrives -> Blessing -> Companion Hatching -> Companion Hero), driven by a new data-only CompanionDirector.getCeremonySequence(). The Companion Pose Contract grows to 12 poses for Story Companions (hatching mandatory, used only once); Story Egg grows to 8 poses (+magic); Lumo's own 8-pose set is deliberately left unexpanded (Guardian, not a Story Companion). Random bonding is a single isolated js/magicCard.js function (assignBondedCompanion), permanent once claimed, never re-rolled on cross-device recall (the bond travels with the identity). claim() now fires only after the full ceremony completes, not on nickname-submit, so the sky/companion the child watches form always matches what their card actually gets. The Magic Card becomes the permanent record of the Creator Bond (companion portrait/name/species, derived Stories/Worlds counts; Achievements explicitly deferred). js/companionEngine.js was not touched -- zero lines changed, verified via git diff -- the Companion Runtime stays fully generic with zero hardcoded companion-id branches anywhere. Verified via a new 40-assertion Playwright suite plus full regression across the existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform/Magic-Card/Companion suite (3 stale assertions updated for this sprint's own intentional behavior changes, confirmed via git stash comparison) and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder change; tools/world-builder/ and tools/world-builder-v2/ untouched.
